key_insight: The paper replaces coarse Doeblin coefficients with a nonlinear "Doeblin curve" that captures how Markov kernels contract information at different divergence and power levels, even when the usual coefficient is zero.
engineering_relevance: This gives sharper, non-vacuous guarantees for noisy optimization, reliable noisy computation, and differential privacy analyses in systems where standard contraction bounds are too weak.
method: Introduces Doeblin curves as a divergence-level characterization of multi-way contraction.
Derives a variational characterization of Doeblin coefficients and uses it to bound the new curves.
Extends the framework to power-constrained variants and applies it to optimization, circuits, and privacy settings.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical results with upper/lower bounds and multiple application case studies, but no empirical dataset or benchmark evaluation.
watch_item: The framework is mathematically richer but may require careful estimation or problem-specific tuning of divergence/power parameters in practice.
